name: Albondigas~ Meatball Soup, Mexican Recipe
recipeIngredient: 1 small ripe tomato, seeded and chopped
2 tablespoons fat-free vinaigrette with red bell peppers (store bought)
1/2 cup seltzer water, divided for use
Vegetable cooking spray or 1 teaspoon virgin olive oil
30 ounces stewed tomatoes (2 cans), Mexican spices
1/2 small jalapeno, or less to taste
15 ounces low-fat chicken broth, low salt
3 teaspoons low-fat Monterey Jack cheese, shredded (optional)
recipeInstructions:
- For the Salsa: Combine all ingredients except lettuce. Chill.
- Add lettuce closer to serving time.
- For the Meatballs: Crumble ground turkey breast in a bowl with crackers. Process zucchini and onion using the Fine Shredding disk. Transfer to the bowl. Add garlic powder, Corriander Cohort Blend, and half the seltzer; combine. Add more seltzer as needed to bind and fluff. Shape into 1 to 1-1/4-inch balls. Brown in a large oiled skillet over medium-high until golden or slightly burned for a "grilled" flavor. Do not crowd meatballs. (Tip: Two batches, or second pan.)
- For the Soup Base: Pulse canned tomatoes, garlic clove, and portion of stemmed jalapeno with metal blade in processor to make a chunky sauce. Add more jalapeno to taste.
- Slow Cook (4 hrs on 4 & 5): Transfer sauce to an 4-quart slow cooker (oblong). Set dial to 5; add broth and oregano. Heat. Add meatballs, bring to gentle boil for about 10 minutes. Reset dial to 4; cook covered for 3 hours, then add vegetables. Cook for 45-60 minutes. For tender-crisp zucchini, add during last 10 minutes. Add cilantro near end of cooking time.
- Detail: Julienne carrot into 1/4-inch by 1-1/2 inch sticks. Cut celery lengthwise and then diagonally into 1/4-inch pieces. Trim stem and tip from zucchini; cut into fourths, then cube.
- Pantry: Use canned ready-to-use chopped tomatoes or pre-seasoned ones (preferred flavor of pre-cooked tomatoes). Keep a jar of Corriander Cohort on hand: grind equal parts pan-roasted cumin seed, coriander seed, and black peppercorns.
- To Plate: Place desired amount of corn chips in bowl. Sprinkle with cheese. Ladle soup into serving dishes (about 1-1/2 to 1-2/3 cups broth per serving). Serve with salad or use as relish on top of soup.
Variation ~ Fast Cook (60 mins): Combine sauce and heat gently until boiling. Add meatballs to broth, reduce heat to simmer; cook for 35 minutes. Add carrots and celery for 5 minutes. Add zucchini for 4-5 minutes. Stir in cilantro and cook about 2 minutes before serving.
Description: Zucchini paired with a bright tomato sauce clings to light, fluffy meatballs. Mild to moderate pepper heat. Takes half a day in slow cooker or one hour on stove top. Serves 2 to 4; contains 19.5% CFF (352 calories and 8.4g fat) without the optional corn chips.
